mechanism_narrative: A falling 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS lowers discount rates applied to future earnings and raises equity duration values, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the rate trend is down.
failure_condition: Reject if falling 3-month FEDFUNDS trends produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations or Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above a high-slack threshold, expected earnings growth deteriorates and risk premia rise, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE crossings above 7.5% are followed by nonnegative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward cases.

mechanism_narrative: Joint gains in RSAFS and PAYEMS during elevated unemployment signal a real-demand recovery with improving household income and spending, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both post positive 3-month changes while UNRATE remains above 7%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value is 415695 and UNRATE is 7.8
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with elevated labor slack
failure_condition: Reject if the joint recovery trigger has lower average 20-day S&P 500 returns than unconditional returns in walk-forward testing or if the interaction term has the wrong sign in more than 60% of rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in INDPRO below a contraction threshold signals weakening goods output and operating leverage pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as cyclicals reprice.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO crossings below 98 are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered observations or the hit rate is not distinguishable from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: HOUST crossing above a depressed housing threshold signals improving credit-sensitive demand and future construction activity, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST crossings above 800 have median 60-day S&P 500 returns below zero or average triggered returns fail to exceed unconditional returns by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: Fast CPIAUCSL inflation while FEDFUNDS remains near zero signals policy behind the inflation curve and raises future tightening risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 3% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value is 231.025 and FEDFUNDS is 0.16
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS trigger is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the interaction coefficient is not negative in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDPC1 growth over two quarters signals broad demand expansion and supports aggregate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive two-quarter GDPC1 growth does not produce higher average 60-day S&P 500 returns than nonpositive growth periods or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in PAYEMS while FEDFUNDS is already near the zero lower bound signals labor income weakness with limited conventional policy offset, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change turns negative while FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot value is 134021.0 and FEDFUNDS is 0.16
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-FEDFUNDS trigger is followed by nonnegative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered observations or if the joint trigger adds no forecast power over PAYEMS alone.
